case_b.text: Situation: Eric has just finished eating a pricey meal at a restaurant. Intention: Eric wants to save money on his food. Action: Eric performs a dine and dash leaving without paying the bill. Consequence: The service staff and business lose money.
case_b.outward_act_summary: Eric performs a dine and dash leaving without paying the bill.
case_b.motive_summary: Eric wants to save money on his food.
case_b.consequence_summary: The service staff and business lose money.
case_b.rule_summary: You should pay for the food you eat.

gold.adjudication_note: Both cases keep the same stated intention, so Task B should stay Same. Case B is more morally problematic because it violates the governing norm while case A does not.

case_a.text: Situation: Martha is preparing a presentation for a major marketing meeting. Intention: Martha wants to get input from multiple people with expertise. Action: Martha talks to various experts and asks them if she can include their ideas in her presentation. Consequence: Martha's presentation goes beautifully, and she is praised for the in depth research she has done.

case_a.consequence_summary: Martha's presentation goes beautifully, and she is praised for the in depth research she has done.

case_a.text: Situation: Bobby finds himself running out of money after losing his job with his rent coming due. Intention: Bobby wants to attain some money in order to pay his bills. Action: Bobby sells his laptop on Craigslist and puts away the money in his bank account. Consequence: Bobby is able to pay his rent with the proceeds from selling his laptop.
case_a.outward_act_summary: Bobby sells his laptop on Craigslist and puts away the money in his bank account.
case_a.motive_summary: Bobby wants to attain some money in order to pay his bills.
case_a.consequence_summary: Bobby is able to pay his rent with the proceeds from selling his laptop.
case_a.rule_summary: You should never scam anyone out of their money

case_b.text: Situation: Bobby finds himself running out of money after losing his job with his rent coming due. Intention: Bobby wants to attain some money in order to pay his bills. Action: Bobby decides to sell items on craigslist and and as he is meeting somebody to sell his laptop he snatches the money and drives off. Consequence: The buyer reports Bobby's license plate to the police, and he is arrested.
